playbook.intent: Engagement ending → client in-house team operates the product alone with code, runbooks, ADRs, on-call rota, training videos, and a 90-day defect SLA; vendor exits with a referenceable signed acceptance letter.
playbook.scope: Three-week structured handover from the outsource vendor to a client in-house team. Output: code + runbooks + ADRs in client hands, on-call rota populated, training videos recorded, 90-day defect SLA active, and signed acceptance letter. No Slack call to the outgoing dev for 30 days after the cutover.

success-criteria.criterion: Architecture decision records up-to-date and in client repo
Living-documentation refreshed for every active module
Runbooks for top-10 operations (deploy, rollback, incident, backup)
On-call rota populated for the first 90 days
Training videos covering top user-facing flows
Signed acceptance letter from client sponsor
Vendor-side support contact decommissioned after 90-day warranty

stage.name: Receiving team readiness
stage.intent: Profile the in-house team and shape the handover to their gaps.

tasks.task: Map team skills vs deployed stack
Run gap-analysis on top operational scenarios
Sketch onboarding 30-60-90 day plan for the receiving team
Negotiate hold-back resources from vendor side (escalation contacts)

stage.decision-gate: Advance once the in-house team accepts the 30-60-90 plan AND vendor escalation rota is signed. Otherwise re-scope handover.

stage.name: Documentation deep-clean
stage.intent: Burn down doc debt before knowledge walks out the door.

tasks.task: Audit ADRs; backfill missing decisions
Refresh design docs to match current state
Adopt living-documentation conventions (AGENTS.md per module)
Document scope boundaries explicitly so the in-house team knows what was NOT built

stage.decision-gate: Advance when a new hire on the receiving team can read the docs and answer top-10 system questions unaided.

stage.name: Ops, monitoring, runbooks
stage.intent: Hand over the production levers, not just the source.

tasks.task: Set up Grafana dashboards aligned to NFRs
Migrate or share Prometheus alerts
Document API monitoring, logging, alerting
Rotate secrets; client team holds the keys
Verify backup + DR rotation (AWS monitoring DR)
Renew TLS automation in client CI/CD

outputs.output: Top-10 runbooks (deploy, rollback, restore, scale, etc.)

stage.decision-gate: Advance when client on-call can complete a full incident drill unaided.

stage.name: Knowledge transfer sessions
stage.intent: Live walkthroughs + recordings the in-house team can rewatch.

tasks.task: Record top user-facing flows as training videos
Pair an in-house dev with a vendor dev per module
Run two architecture deep-dives with the receiving lead
Hand over AI-agent prompts and constraints used during build

stage.decision-gate: Advance when each module has ≥1 in-house dev who has merged a PR unaided.

stage.name: Cutover & acceptance
stage.intent: Sign the closure paperwork; activate the warranty SLA.

tasks.task: Run benefits-realisation review with client sponsor
Project closure ceremony (vendor + client)
Stand up 90-day warranty SLA + intake channel
Capture testimonial / referenceability
Decommission vendor access (laptops, accounts, VPN)

stage.decision-gate: Engagement closes only with a signed acceptance letter AND zero open critical defects.

extra.value: ['Continued maintenance — handled by warranty SLA, not staff augmentation', Hiring the in-house team — client owns, New feature delivery during the handover window — frozen]
[Engagement scope is complete or end-date locked, Client in-house team identified and at least partially staffed, Lessons-learned register exists for the engagement]

['Treating handover as a deck — the doc burns in a fortnight, the runbooks live forever', Holding back tribal knowledge to preserve future work — kills referenceability, Skipping AI-agent prompt handover; the receiving team will reinvent unsafe defaults]
['Did the in-house team merge a real PR before cutover?', 'Could an on-call rotation answer a 3am page without phoning the vendor?', 'Did the sponsor sign the acceptance letter — not just the PM?']

{human: faion get-content handover-to-client-in-house-team --format md, agent: faion get-content handover-to-client-in-house-team --format context, json: faion get-content handover-to-client-in-house-team --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft for F-064 P4 outsource-specialist persona, global angle'}]
